cites.quote: Every deed of the son of Adam is for him except fasting; it is for Me, and I shall reward for it.

Islam is built upon five: the testimony that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah and that Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah, establishing prayer, giving zakat, performing Hajj, and fasting Ramadan.
Whoever fasts Ramadan out of faith and in the hope of reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.
Whoever fasts six days after the Fitr will have completed the year, for whoever does a good deed will have the reward of ten like it.
Whoever fasts Ramadan then follows it with six days of Shawwal, it is as if he fasted for a lifetime.
Whoever fasts the month of Ramadan and then follows it with six days of fasting in the month of Shawwal, it will be as if he has fasted for the entire year.

cites.intro: Abu Qatada (ra) reported the Messenger Muhammad (pbuh) said:
cites.quote: I seek from Allah that fasting on the day of 'Arafa may atone for the sins of the preceding and the coming years, and I seek from Allah that fasting on the day of Ashura may atone for the sins of the preceding year.

fasting-reason-first-9-dhu-al-hijjah.title: First nine days of Dhu al-Hijjah

cites.intro: One of the wives (ra) of the Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) said:
cites.quote: The Messenger of Allah Muhammad used to fast the first nine days of Dhu al-Hijjah, the day of Ashura, and three days of every month…

cites.intro: It was narrated from Hunaidah bin Khalid (ra) that his wife (ra) said:
cites.quote: The Prophet used to fast on the day of 'Ashura, nine days of Dhul-Hijjah and three days each month: The first Monday of the month, and two Thursday.

cites.intro: The Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) advised Abu Dharr (ra):
cites.quote: If you fast any part of the month, then fast the thirteenth, fourteenth and fifteenth.
And it is sufficient for you to fast three days in a month, as the reward of a good deed is multiplied ten times, so it will be like fasting throughout the year.

cites.intro: bu Qatada (ar) reported  the Messenger of Allah (pbuh) said:
cites.quote: Fasting three days every month and that of Ramadan every year is a perpetual fasting.

cites.intro: The Messenger of Allah said Abu Dharr:
cites.quote: Fasting three days of every month is like fasting for a lifetime.

cites.intro: It was narrated from Jarir bin 'Abdullah (ra) that the Prophet (pbuh) said:
cites.quote: Fasting three days of each month is fasting for a lifetime, and the shining days of Al-Bid, the thirteenth, fourteenth and fifteenth.
My close friend (the Prophet Muhammad) advised me to do three things: to fast three days of every month, pray two rak‘at of Duha, and pray Witr before sleeping.

cites.intro: Abu Al-Darda' (ra) reported the Prophet (pbuh) said:
cites.quote: My friend instructed me to observe three practices which I never leave: he instructed me to fast three days every month, and not to sleep but after observing the witr, and to observe supererogatory prayer in the forenoon while traveling and while resident.

cites.intro: This specific statement is narrated by Abu Hurairah (ra):
cites.quote: Deeds are presented on Mondays and Thursdays, and I love that my deeds be presented while I am fasting.

cites.intro: Usamah bin Zaid (ra) reported the Messenger (pbuh) said:
cites.quote: Those are two days in which deeds are shown to the Lord of the worlds, and I like my deeds to be shown when I am fasting.

cites.intro: The Prophet (pbuh) was asked by Abu Qatada al-Ansari (ra) about fasting on Monday, and he said:
cites.quote: It was the day on which I was born. on which I was commissioned with prophethood or revelation was sent to me,
The Prophet used to be keen to fast on Mondays and Thursdays.
